---
title: "Build with us"
description: "Add a new feature, component or workflow"
order: 0
listing:
  id: contributing
  type: table
  contents:
    - getting-started.qmd
    - project-structure.qmd
    - development-workflow.qmd
    - creating-components.qmd
    - creating-pipelines.qmd
    - testing.qmd
    - pull-requests.qmd
  fields: [title, description]
  field-display-names:
    title: "Step"
    description: "What it covers"
  sort: false
  filter-ui: false
  sort-ui: false
---

OpenPipeline is open-source. Whether you want to fix a typo, report a bug, or add
a whole new workflow, contributions are welcome.

## Ways to contribute

You don't have to write code to help.

- **Report a bug or request a feature.** Open an issue on the relevant package
  repository (see [Getting started](getting-started.qmd) for the list). A clear
  bug report with steps to reproduce is a real contribution.
- **Improve the documentation.** Every page has room to be clearer. Spot
  something wrong or missing? Open an issue or a pull request.
- **Review or discuss.** Comment on open pull requests and issues, or help answer
  questions from other users.
- **Contribute code.** Add a [component](creating-components.qmd) or a
  [pipeline](creating-pipelines.qmd), or fix an existing one. The steps below
  walk you through it end to end.

## How a code contribution flows

Every code change follows the same path, regardless of the package:

```
fork & clone  →  develop locally  →  test  →  pull request  →  Viash Hub publishes
```

You work on a branch in your fork, build and test locally with Viash, then open a
pull request against `main`. Once a maintainer merges it, [Viash
Hub](project-structure.qmd#sec-versioning) builds and publishes the package with
the next release. The steps below follow that path in order.

:::{#contributing}
:::

## Where to get help

Stuck? Open an issue on the package repository, or start a discussion on
[GitHub](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio). Questions are welcome, including
"is this the right place for my change?" before you start.

## Code of conduct

OpenPipeline adopts the
[Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/). We pledge to keep
the community welcoming, inclusive, and harassment-free, and by taking part you
agree to uphold it. Please read the full
[Code of Conduct](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
before participating.
